docs.title: React Hooks - useState
docs.snippet: useState is a React Hook that lets you add state to functional components. Returns an array with the current state value and a function to update it.

docs.title: React Hooks - useEffect
docs.snippet: useEffect is a React Hook that lets you perform side effects in function components. It accepts a function and a dependency array.

docs.title: React Hooks - useContext
docs.snippet: useContext is a React Hook that lets you read and subscribe to context from your component. Returns the context value passed to the nearest Provider.

docs.title: React Conditional Rendering
docs.snippet: In React, you can create distinct components that encapsulate behavior you need. Then, you can render only some of them, depending on the state of your application.

docs.title: React Lists and Keys
docs.snippet: Often you'll want to display multiple similar components from a collection of data. You can use JavaScript array methods to manipulate an array of data.

docs.title: Express Middleware
docs.snippet: Middleware functions are functions that have access to the request object (req), the response object (res), and the next middleware function in the application's request-response cycle.

docs.title: Express Routing
docs.snippet: Routing refers to how an application's endpoints respond to client requests. You define routes using methods of the Express app object that correspond to HTTP methods.

docs.title: Node.js fs Module
docs.snippet: The fs module provides an API for interacting with the file system. You can read, write, update, delete, and rename files using this module.

docs.title: Node.js Async/Await
docs.snippet: Async functions allow you to write promise-based code as if it were synchronous. The await keyword pauses execution until the promise resolves.

docs.title: Node.js Events
docs.snippet: Much of the Node.js core API is built around an idiomatic asynchronous event-driven architecture. Objects that emit events are instances of EventEmitter class.

docs.title: Supabase Authentication
docs.snippet: Supabase provides built-in authentication with support for email/password, passwordless sign-ins, OAuth providers, and more. Use the Supabase client library to handle authentication.

docs.title: Supabase Realtime
docs.snippet: Supabase Realtime allows you to subscribe to database changes in real-time. Listen for INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE events on your tables using WebSockets.

docs.title: Supabase Storage
docs.snippet: Supabase Storage is a solution for managing user-generated content. Upload, download, and delete files from your Supabase storage buckets.

docs.title: Supabase PostgreSQL
docs.snippet: Supabase is a hosted PostgreSQL database with real-time capabilities. Use SQL queries or the JavaScript client to interact with your database.

docs.title: Supabase RLS Policies
docs.snippet: Row Level Security enables you to write PostgreSQL policies that restrict which rows users can access. Essential for multi-tenant applications.

docs.title: JavaScript Array Methods
docs.snippet: Arrays have built-in methods like map(), filter(), reduce(), find(), forEach(). These functional programming methods are essential for data manipulation.

docs.title: JavaScript Promises
docs.snippet: Promises represent the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation. Chain with .then() or use with async/await.

docs.title: JavaScript Template Literals
docs.snippet: Template literals are string literals that allow embedded expressions. Use backticks and ${} for interpolation instead of concatenation.

docs.title: JavaScript Destructuring
docs.snippet: Destructuring allows you to unpack values from objects and arrays. Use curly braces for objects and square brackets for arrays.

docs.title: JavaScript Arrow Functions
docs.snippet: Arrow functions provide a concise syntax for function expressions. They don't have their own 'this' binding and are perfect for callbacks.
